---
title: "AI Trading Systems"
description: "How LLMs and AI agents could support market decision-making — with the risk controls that make it credible."
toc: true
---

This section develops the research direction behind my proposed PhD: how large
language models and AI agents might support market decision-making. The emphasis
is on **transparency and control** — an agent should be a testable, auditable
component of a system, never an unsupervised black box placing trades.

## Topics

- What is an AI agent?
- LLMs versus conventional machine-learning models
- Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
- Sentiment analysis and news interpretation
- Tool-using agents
- Multi-agent systems
- Agent memory and planning
- AI risk controls
- Hallucination risk when LLMs meet financial data
- Why an LLM should never directly place trades without hard controls

## A proposed multi-agent architecture

A concrete system I want to study — deliberately decomposed so each component can
be tested and evaluated on its own, rather than trusting a single model end to end:

- **Market Regime Agent** — classifies the current trend, volatility, and liquidity state.
- **News Agent** — reads and summarises the prevailing market narrative from text.
- **Risk Agent** — sets and adjusts position size within hard, pre-defined limits.
- **Execution Agent** — manages entries and exits against the chosen signal.
- **Review Agent** — evaluates, after the fact, whether each decision was justified given the information available at the time.

The **Review Agent** matters most: without a rigorous, honest evaluation loop, a
system like this is just automation of overconfidence. The
[PhD Research](../phd/index.qmd) section develops the formal proposal around this
architecture.
